name: City of El Paso
description: Largest city in the Borderplex region. Five strategic pillars: Economic Mobility, Public Safety, Infrastructure, Quality of Life, Good Governance. Only 22% satisfaction with living-wage job availability; 31% satisfaction with street conditions.

name: El Paso County
description: Encompasses unincorporated communities with critical infrastructure gaps. Serves colonias and rural areas east and southeast of El Paso with limited water, wastewater, and broadband access.

name: City of Las Cruces
description: Second largest Borderplex anchor city. Six strategic themes: Community Prosperity, Education, Environmental Sustainability, Infrastructure, Communication, Public Safety. Climate Action Plan adopted 2020.

name: Doña Ana County
description: Spans from Las Cruces to the Texas border. Contains significant agricultural economy, rural water challenges, and unincorporated communities. Home to NMSU and the Santa Teresa port of entry.

name: City of Socorro
description: Fast-growing community in the Lower Valley. Faces rapid population growth with inadequate infrastructure, limited water/wastewater capacity, and constrained fiscal resources for public safety.

name: City of Horizon City
description: Rapidly expanding eastern suburb of El Paso. Infrastructure expansion cannot keep pace with residential growth. Limited commercial tax base constrains public service delivery.

name: City of San Elizario
description: Historic community with deep cultural significance. Faces aging water/wastewater infrastructure, limited broadband access, and need for heritage tourism development as an economic engine.

name: Town of Anthony, TX
description: Small border community straddling the TX/NM state line. Economic development constrained by limited governance capacity and cross-state jurisdictional complexity.

name: City of Anthony, NM
description: Twin community to Anthony TX. Agricultural economy transitioning under water stress. Unique binational/bi-state identity creates both challenges and opportunities for cross-border collaboration.

name: Tornillo CDP
description: Unincorporated farming community in far eastern El Paso County. Severe water infrastructure deficits, no municipal governance structure, dependent on county and special district services.

name: Fabens CDP
description: Agricultural community with colonia-adjacent residential areas. Aging water treatment systems, limited workforce development infrastructure, and irrigation-dependent economy facing climate stress.

name: Vado CDP
description: Unincorporated community south of Las Cruces near the Santa Teresa port of entry. Positioned for logistics growth but lacks basic infrastructure and workforce readiness.

name: Ciudad Juárez
description: Largest manufacturing center on the U.S.-Mexico border. Over 330 maquiladoras employing 300,000+ workers. Critical supply chain and labor market linkages to El Paso. Air quality, water sharing, and cross-border mobility challenges.
